name: Peñón de Pez Trail
name_original: Castillo de Capilla desde Ermita de San Sebastián — ida y vuelta
description: Hike the easy 2.6-mile Peñón de Pez Trail to enjoy panoramic views of Capilla and explore the historic Castillo de Capilla.
region_intro: Hiking around Capilla offers a diverse landscape for outdoor enthusiasts. The region features a mix of rolling hills and more challenging terrain, providing varied elevation changes. Hikers can expect to encounter areas with dense vegetation and open vistas, characteristic of the local environment. The network of trails caters to different skill levels, from gentle walks to more strenuous ascents. Best hiking trails around Capilla The most popular hiking route is Peñón del Pez – circular from Peñalsordo via Capilla, a difficult 5.9 miles (9.5 km) trail that takes 3 hours 2 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain and panoramic views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Peñón del Pez Trail, a moderate 8.8 miles (14.2 km) path. This trail winds through varied terrain, offering a balanced challenge. Local hikers also love the Castillo de Chillón — circular desde Chillón, a 6.1 miles (9.8 km) trail leading through historical landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 54 minutes. Hiking around Capilla is defined by rolling hills, varied elevations, and a mix of open and vegetated areas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to difficult ascents. The routes in Capilla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from 3 reviews. More than 19 hikers have used komoot to explore Capilla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of hiking terrain can I expect around Capilla?
A: Hiking around Capilla offers a diverse landscape, characterized by rolling hills and varied elevations. You'll find a mix of dense vegetation and open vistas. For example, in the Capilla Peak area of New Mexico, trails often lead to panoramic views from summits, while Capilla del Señor in Argentina provides tranquil, gentle walks through nature reserves with lush vegetation.

Q: Are there hiking options for different skill levels in Capilla?
A: Yes, the Capilla area caters to various skill levels. The network of trails includes options from gentle walks to more strenuous ascents. According to komoot data, there are 4 easy, 6 moderate, and 3 difficult hiking routes available, ensuring something for everyone.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available in Capilla?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. A notable one is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991293513/penon-del-pez-circular-from-penalsordo-via-capilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón del Pez – circular from Peñalsordo via Capilla&lt;/a&gt;, which is a difficult 5.9-mile trail offering panoramic views. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1296895077/castillo-de-chillon-circular-desde-chillon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Chillón — circular desde Chillón&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 6.1-mile route through historical landscapes.

Q: Can I find trails with good viewpoints in Capilla?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in the Capilla region offer excellent viewpoints. For instance, the Capilla Peak area is known for stunning panoramic views in all directions from its summit. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3687215" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Peñón del Pez&lt;/a&gt;, which is a popular highlight among hikers.

Q: Are there historical sites or landmarks to see while hiking?
A: Yes, the Capilla region has several historical sites and landmarks. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3687196" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Capilla&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5906212" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chillón Castle&lt;/a&gt;. In the Capilla Peak area, there's also a historic fire lookout that offers a glimpse into the region's past.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Capilla?
A: For families seeking gentler outdoor activities, the Capilla del Señor area in Argentina offers tranquil environments ideal for relaxation and gentle walks. The Arroyo de la Cruz Nature Reserve features walkways amidst lush vegetation, perfect for picnics and observing flora and fauna, accompanied by bird songs. The Arco Iris Walk is also a pleasant natural escape.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Capilla?
A: The Capilla area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from 3 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, which includes rolling hills and diverse landscapes, as well as the options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging ascents.

Q: How long do hikes typically take in the Capilla area?
A: Hike durations vary depending on the route and your pace. For example, the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991293513/penon-del-pez-circular-from-penalsordo-via-capilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón del Pez – circular from Peñalsordo via Capilla&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 3 hours 2 minutes to complete. A moderate route like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1296895077/castillo-de-chillon-circular-desde-chillon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Chillón — circular desde Chillón&lt;/a&gt; is often completed in about 2 hours 54 minutes.

Q: Are there any trails that feature natural caves?
A: Yes, in the Capilla Peak area of New Mexico, hikers can explore small caves, adding another element of natural exploration to their outdoor experience.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Capilla?
A: The best time to hike in Capilla depends on the specific region. For areas like Capilla Peak in New Mexico, spring and fall generally offer pleasant temperatures for hiking. For the more tranquil walks in Capilla del Señor, Argentina, the mild seasons are ideal for enjoying the nature reserves.

Q: Are there any long-distance or more challenging hikes available?
A: Yes, for those seeking a greater challenge, Capilla offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1294250828/sierra-de-las-poyatas-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Las Poyatas Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 13.5-mile route with significant elevation gain, providing a strenuous and rewarding experience.
tips: Spectacular view of Capilla from the walk to the castle above the town. On a clear day you can see it for miles.

It was erected by the Muslims, and during their occupation it was already a strategic enclave in communications between Mérida, Seville, Córdoba, Almadén and Toledo. The town of Capilla and its castle were conquered in 1226 by King Ferdinand III of Castilla y León, who donated it to the Knights Templar in 1236, after the conquest of Córdoba.
Its structure has survived to the present day, although its condition is in ruins.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Peñón de Pez Trail?
faqs.answer: The initial section of the trail, leading up to a tunnel, is paved and offers a gentle uphill climb. Beyond the tunnel, the terrain changes significantly, becoming narrow, steep, and rocky with loose stones that can be slippery. You may encounter sections that require using your hands for balance and short climbs, often aided by chains and ropes.

faqs.question: What are the key highlights or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers beautiful views of the Calpe marina and the city below from its early stages. From the summit, you'll be treated to expansive and amazing coastal vistas. Specific points of interest include the Panoramic View of Capilla and the historic Castillo de Capilla.

faqs.question: Is the Peñón de Pez Trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: The first part of the trail, up to the tunnel, is considered easy, family-friendly, and suitable for almost everyone. However, beyond the tunnel, the trail becomes moderate to challenging and is officially recommended for adults only due to its steep, rocky, and exposed sections.

faqs.question: Is the Peñón de Pez Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs on a leash are permitted throughout the entire trail.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or reservation to hike the Peñón de Pez Trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, as it is a natural park, access often requires a reservation due to a daily limit on visitors, such as 300 people per day. It's advisable to check for current reservation requirements before your visit.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: While the trail can be hiked year-round, it's best to avoid hiking during or immediately after rain due to increased slipperiness on the rocky sections. Also, be aware that the sun can be intense, even outside of summer, with minimal shade available on the upper parts of the trail.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Peñón de Pez Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail typically takes around 1 hour and 15 minutes to complete, covering a distance of approximately 4.1 kilometers.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a hike on the Peñón de Pez Trail?
faqs.answer: Sturdy shoes with good grip are essential, especially for the more challenging sections beyond the tunnel. Given the intense sun and minimal shade, bringing plenty of water, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and appropriate clothing is highly recommended.

faqs.question: Where does the Peñón de Pez Trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for the Peñón de Pez Trail are not available, trails of this nature often have designated parking areas near the trailhead. It's recommended to check local signage or official park information for the most accurate parking and trailhead access points.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Sendero del Peñón de Pez, covering approximately 79% of its length.

faqs.question: Are there any specific safety considerations for the trail?
faqs.answer: Beyond the tunnel, the trail becomes challenging with steep, narrow, and exposed sections. It's advised to avoid hiking during or after rain due to increased slipperiness. Individuals uncomfortable with heights or exposed paths may find this section particularly challenging. Always wear sturdy shoes with good grip.
